calendarNotes:
Long Beach Unified School District serves California's second-largest city and follows a calendar broadly aligned with the Los Angeles region, though exact dates consistently differ from neighboring LAUSD by one to two weeks. In 2025-2026, the first day of school is August 26, 2025 — approximately ten days after LAUSD begins. Spring Break runs April 6–10, 2025, compared to LAUSD's late-March break. Families coordinating schedules with relatives or childcare providers in LAUSD should note these differences each year, as the gap can shift.

Long Beach's coastal location gives it a noticeably different climate than the inland San Gabriel and San Fernando Valleys — marine layer keeps summers cooler and more overcast, and the area rarely experiences the extreme heat events that affect Fresno or even parts of eastern Los Angeles County. This means back-to-school preparation in late August is more manageable than in interior California.

LBUSD operates one of the stronger magnet and choice school systems in California, with International Baccalaureate programs, performing arts magnets, and STEM academies spread across elementary, middle, and high school levels. Choice school applications typically open in October or November for the following year — well before spring break and before many families start thinking about enrollment. Families new to the district should check LBUSD's school choice portal in the fall, not the spring.

Families living near the LAUSD-LBUSD boundary — which runs through parts of South Los Angeles, Compton, and Carson — should verify their assigned district using their home address. School calendars, start times, and holiday schedules may differ even for households on the same street.
